name: github-automation
description: Automate GitHub repositories, issues, pull requests, branches, CI/CD, and permissions via Rube MCP (Composio). Manage code workflows, review PRs, search code, and handle deployments programmatically.
capabilities: Create, list, and manage GitHub issues with labels, assignees, and comments
Search, review, merge, and manage pull requests with CI status checks
Create repositories, branches, and manage branch protection rules
Search code and commits across repositories with advanced filters
Trigger GitHub Actions workflows and monitor deployment status
Manage repository collaborators and check user permissions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 190 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only Markdown describing GitHub API usage via Rube MCP. No executable code is present. Backtick patterns are Markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate MCP server endpoint for user configuration.
valueStatement: Streamline your GitHub operations by automating issues, pull requests, branches, and CI/CD pipelines. This skill integrates with Rube MCP to execute GitHub API actions programmatically through Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
userTitle: Automate GitHub workflows and repository management
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection to be configured and active
GitHub OAuth authentication required through Rube Manage Connections
Actions limited to authenticated user's repository access permissions
Cannot execute operations on private repos without proper authorization

useCases.title: Development Team Lead
useCases.description: Automate PR review workflows by listing open pull requests, checking CI status, and merging approved changes with explicit confirmation.

useCases.title: Open Source Maintainer
useCases.description: Manage incoming issues and feature requests by creating labeled issues, assigning contributors, and tracking progress across multiple repositories.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer
useCases.description: Monitor and trigger CI/CD pipelines, check deployment statuses, and manage branch protection rules for production branches.

promptTemplates.title: List Open Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: List all open issues in the repository owner/repo and summarize their status.

promptTemplates.title: Create Feature Request Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue in owner/repo with title 'Feature: X' and body describing the feature requirements. Add labels 'enhancement' and assign to user 'devname'.

promptTemplates.title: Review and Merge PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Find open pull requests in owner/repo by author 'contributor'. Check the CI status for each PR. If checks pass, prepare merge summary for my approval.
promptTemplates.scenario: PR review workflow with safety checks

promptTemplates.title: Trigger Deployment Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: List available workflows in owner/repo. Trigger the 'deploy.yml' workflow on the 'main' branch with inputs for environment 'production'. Monitor the deployment status.

bestPractices: Always verify CI check status before merging any pull request
Request explicit user confirmation for destructive operations like merge or delete
Use pagination parameters to iterate through large result sets completely
antiPatterns: Merging pull requests without checking CI status or merge conflicts
Assuming labels or assignees were set without verifying user permissions
Deleting repositories or branches without explicit user confirmation

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and do I need it?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is an MCP server that provides GitHub API access. You must add https://rube.app/mcp to your MCP client configuration and complete GitHub OAuth authentication before using this skill.

faq.question: Can this skill access private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but only repositories where the authenticated GitHub user has appropriate permissions. Access is controlled by your GitHub OAuth token scopes.

faq.question: How does pagination work for listing operations?
faq.answer: List endpoints return paginated results. Use page and per_page parameters (max 100 per page). Iterate until you receive fewer results than requested.

faq.question: Why did my issue creation silently drop labels?
faq.answer: Labels, assignees, and milestones require push access to set. If you lack permission, these fields are silently ignored by the GitHub API.

faq.question: Can I merge a pull request automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires explicit user confirmation before any merge operation. This is a safety measure to prevent accidental merges.

faq.question: What happens if a branch is unprotected?
faq.answer: The GET branch protection endpoint returns 404 for unprotected branches. Treat this as no protection rules applied to that branch.
